candidate_problems.text: How can counterfactual reach be operationalized so it is empirically distinguishable from neighboring capacities like robustness, model-based planning, causal reasoning, and mere memorized policy libraries? The conjecture gives load-bearing invariants, but it remains open what concrete tests, metrics, or formal criteria would show that a system truly has a wide, organized, compositional intervention space with policy portability and pre-contact error correction rather than a disguised bundle of narrower abilities.

What is the right formal measure of the 'breadth-and-depth' of actionable world-variants, and how should bounded degradation, compactness of representation, and structure of the variant space trade off against one another? The conjecture treats counterfactual reach as a structural capacity measure, but leaves unresolved which mathematical objects capture its size and organization, what counts as nontrivial control structure, and how to prevent trivial inflation by arbitrary redescriptions or by counting irrelevant variants.

Under deeper mechanistic redescription, when does counterfactual reach remain a legitimate higher-level invariant rather than dissolve into lower-level descriptions of search, prediction, or control? This raises an unresolved explanatory question about reduction: what exactly must be preserved across levels for the concept to be real, and what kinds of mechanistic decomposition would show that the apparent reach was only metaphorical?
